result:
This is a massive terminal-based simulation engine (`life.py`, ~48K lines) with 84+ simulation modes spanning cellular automata, physics, fluids, fractals, and more.

**Proposed goal: Add a "Matrix Digital Rain" simulation mode.**

The iconic falling green character streams from The Matrix — columns of cascading random characters at varying speeds and brightnesses, with leading bright heads and fading tails. It's a perfect fit for a terminal-based simulator, visually striking, and would bolster the "Audio & Visual" category which currently only has 2 modes. The implementation would include configurable density, speed variation per column, character randomization, and color gradient fading.
